masthead.title_html: Reason Your Way Through Brawl Stars
masthead.title_plain: Reason Your Way Through Brawl Stars
masthead.dek: Twenty-two questions on Supercell's brawler. We skip the patch-to-patch stat memorizing and stick to what you can reason out — unique mechanics, lore, firsts, and the way the modes work. Narrow it down, place your bet, then tap to check.
masthead.footer_html: Brawl Stars facts verified to stable sources (Wiki, Supercell, Liquipedia). As of 2024-25. The topic is reasoning-thin past here — this is its honest ceiling.

plan.note: Built holding the bar — only reasoning-driven, stable-fact questions; volatile balance stats deliberately excluded, which caps the set near 22.

questions.q: Every brand-new Brawl Stars account begins with the same brawler already unlocked. Who is it?
questions.detail: Shelly, a shotgun brawler, is the default starter handed to all new players.
questions.why: Of the ~90 brawlers, only one is the guaranteed starter.
questions.more: Shelly and Nita are treated as the game's original mascots.

questions.q: When Brawl Stars first launched, only two of its brawlers were Legendary rarity — and one of them is a little cactus. Name him.
questions.detail: Spike was one of just two Legendaries in the original beta roster (the other was Crow).
questions.why: Players assume the launch roster was all 'basic' brawlers; a Legendary cactus shipped on day one.
questions.more: Spike's attack throws a cactus that bursts into six spikes.

questions.q: Most brawlers are earned slowly, but one melee fighter who heals a little with every punch was handed out free as a 2020 holiday gift. Who is he?
questions.detail: Edgar's punches lifesteal ~35% of damage; he was a free Brawlidays 2020 gift.
questions.why: A free brawler is usually a weak common; Edgar is an aggressive Epic assassin, defeating the 'free = basic' reflex.
questions.more: His Super lets him leap over any wall with a speed boost.

questions.q: Most Supers push, blast, or summon. One genie-themed brawler instead shoots a magic hand that GRABS an enemy and yanks them across the map to him. Who is he?
questions.detail: Gene's Super fires a hand from his lamp that pulls a struck enemy to him, smashing walls on the way.
questions.why: Knockback and dashes are common; a pull-the-enemy-in effect is rare and distinctive to Gene.
questions.more: His Hypercharge splits the hand into three.

questions.q: One fragile robot brawler uses his Super by popping off his OWN HEAD and sending it chasing the nearest enemy like a homing bomb. Who is he?
questions.detail: Tick detaches his head as a homing explosive Super; he is also the lowest-health brawler in the game.
questions.why: Throwing your own head as a homing weapon is a one-of-a-kind, counterintuitive mechanic.
questions.more: He lobs mines as his normal attack.

questions.q: Almost every brawler can fire while standing still. One shovel-swinging brawler is different: he can't land his basic attack at all unless he DASHES forward. Who is he?
questions.detail: Mortis's attack is a forward dash-and-swing; standing still, he can't deal his normal damage.
questions.why: A brawler who must relocate every time he attacks defeats the 'aim and shoot' reflex.
questions.more: His dash is cut short if it hits a wall.

questions.q: Among the original launch brawlers, one guitar-playing healer can aim his Super at TEAMMATES to restore their health instead of harming enemies. Who is he?
questions.detail: Poco's wide musical Super heals himself and allies; a Star Power makes his attack heal too.
questions.why: New players assume every attack only hurts enemies; pointing a Super at friends to heal them is the twist.
questions.more: Poco is a Rare brawler from the 2017 beta roster.

questions.q: Hiding in a bush usually keeps you safe, but one brawler can summon a bear that SNIFFS OUT and chases enemies out of the bushes. Who summons that bear?
questions.detail: Nita's Super summons Bruce, a bear that detects brawlers hidden in bushes and pursues them.
questions.why: Bush-hiding is core safety in Brawl Stars; a pet that actively hunts bush-campers is a distinctive counter.
questions.more: Only one Bruce can exist at a time.

questions.q: Barley tosses bottles and Dynamike chucks dynamite, both arcing OVER walls to hit hidden enemies. What class do brawlers who lob projectiles over cover belong to?
questions.detail: Throwers like Barley, Dynamike, and Tick are the Artillery class, defined by lobbing over obstacles.
questions.why: Many players call them 'throwers' and don't know the official class; the arc-over-walls behavior is the tell.
questions.more: Brawl Stars has seven classes in all.

questions.q: Brock, Belle, and Bonnie all want to sit far away and pick enemies off from long range, never brawling up close. What class are these snipers?
questions.detail: Marksmen are the long-range, low-health snipers who win by keeping distance.
questions.why: Players sort by character, not class; the shared snipe-from-afar role is the routing cue.
questions.more: Marksmen are typically fragile, so positioning matters most.

questions.q: Gale was the very first brawler of a special Brawl-Pass rarity that Supercell later ABOLISHED, scattering its brawlers into other tiers. What was that rarity called?
questions.detail: Gale launched as the first Chromatic brawler (Season 1); the tier was later removed and its brawlers redistributed.
questions.why: Players list rarities as if permanent; one was actually retired — a non-obvious history fact.
questions.more: Former Chromatics were folded into Rare, Epic, and Mythic ranks.

questions.q: Brawl Stars spent over a year in a limited soft launch before opening to everyone. In what year did the game finally release worldwide?
questions.detail: Global launch was December 12, 2018, on iOS and Android.
questions.why: The 2017 soft-launch lures players to guess too early.
questions.more: It was Supercell's fifth game.

questions.q: Supercell is based in Finland, but Brawl Stars first opened to the public on iOS in a single other country in 2017. Which country?
questions.detail: The iOS soft launch went live in Canada on June 15, 2017.
questions.why: The Finnish-studio link pulls guesses toward Finland, which only got it later.
questions.more: Finland and Sweden were added in January 2018.

questions.q: Of a brawler's two big kit upgrades — Star Powers (passive) and Gadgets (an active button) — which one was added to the game FIRST?
questions.detail: Star Powers existed from the beta era; Gadgets arrived in the March 2020 'Jacky' update.
questions.why: The tappable Gadget feels core, but the passive Star Power predates it by years.
questions.more: Every brawler got Gadgets in that same update.

questions.q: Most Brawl Stars modes are three-versus-three. Which classic mode instead drops ten players in alone, with no teammates?
questions.detail: Solo Showdown is a 10-player battle royale; last brawler standing wins.
questions.why: Across the ~10 modes, only Showdown breaks the 3v3 pattern with a 10-player free-for-all.
questions.more: It also has a Duos variant (five teams of two).

questions.q: In which classic 3v3 mode can you win without defeating a single opponent, just by smashing the enemy's safe?
questions.detail: Heist tasks each team with destroying the enemy safe while protecting their own.
questions.why: Players assume combat wins; Heist's win condition is structure damage, not eliminations.
questions.more: If neither safe falls, the team that did more damage wins.

questions.q: In Bounty, you earn stars by defeating enemies. How many stars must a team reach to clinch an instant win?
questions.detail: Collecting 20 stars wins outright; otherwise the higher score at the timer wins.
questions.why: Gem Grab's famous '10' bleeds over, making 20 non-obvious.
questions.more: A blue star at center breaks ties.

questions.q: In Gem Grab, a team must hold a certain number of gems through a countdown to win. How many gems?
questions.detail: A team must hold 10 gems through a ~15-second countdown to win.
questions.why: It pairs against Bounty's 20, so the two get swapped.
questions.more: Getting defeated drops your gems and resets the countdown.

questions.q: One of Brawl Stars' most-played modes is a soccer match where you push a ball into a goal. Despite feeling original, it was added the year after global launch. Name it.
questions.detail: Brawl Ball, a soccer-style mode, was introduced in 2019, after the December 2018 launch.
questions.why: Its centrality fools players into pegging it as a launch mode.
questions.more: Kicking the ball doesn't use up your normal attack.

questions.q: An early co-op event called 'Boss Fight' was so demanding it crashed the game and got swapped out. Which surviving three-player co-op event replaced it?
questions.detail: Boss Fight was changed to Robo Rumble, where players defend against waves of robots.
questions.why: Robo Rumble is well known; that it replaced a crash-prone predecessor is the twist.
questions.more: Robo Rumble is a survival-style PvE event.

questions.q: Before 'Power League' and today's 'Ranked,' Brawl Stars' first seasonal competitive ladder had a different name. What was it called?
questions.detail: Power Play (late 2019) was the first competitive ladder; Power League replaced it in 2021, Ranked in 2024.
questions.why: The two newer names dominate memory, masking the original.
questions.more: Power Play used win-loss record; Power League switched to points.

questions.q: The first-ever Brawl Stars World Finals was held in Busan, South Korea in 2019. Which team won it?
questions.detail: Nova Esports won the inaugural 2019 World Finals (prize pool $250,000).
questions.why: Later champions crowd memory; only one org holds the 'first' title.
questions.more: The event featured eight teams.
